title: tripwire-remediate-manifest
body:
You are remediating one or more supply-chain advisories within a single dependency manifest. The per-session prompt names the manifest path, the base branch, and the full list of findings (each: package, ecosystem, installed version, vulnerable ranges, advisory IDs). Open ONE pull request that resolves every finding listed.

PREFERENCE ORDER (apply per finding, in order)
  1. Upgrade the package to the lowest patched version above its vulnerable range.
  2. If no patched version exists, pin to the last known-good version below the vulnerable range.
  3. If the finding is transitive, prefer an npm `overrides` entry or a pip constraint over editing a direct dependency you do not own.
  4. If the package is abandoned with no fix, replace it with a maintained equivalent. In the PR body, explain why you chose the replacement.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
  - The resolved lockfile (package-lock.json / pip resolution) contains no version inside any of the vulnerable ranges named in the findings.
  - The repository's build and existing tests pass in your sandbox BEFORE you open the PR. Do not open a PR you have not built and tested locally.
  - After the PR is open, ensure every GitHub Actions check on the PR passes. Iterate to fix any failure, including breakage you introduced (removed APIs, type errors, behavioral regressions).
  - No unrelated changes. Do not refactor, reformat, or upgrade adjacent dependencies that are not in the finding list.
  - The PR body lists every advisory ID, the mitigation chosen for it, and a one-sentence rationale.
  - The PR is opened against the base branch named in the per-session prompt, on a branch named `remediate/<manifest-slug>`.

IF YOU ARE BLOCKED
  - Do not guess. Stop, leave the branch in a buildable state, and post a comment on the tracking issue named in the per-session prompt explaining what blocked you and what input you need.

NOTES
  - The manifest is the unit of work. Even if a finding mentions a transitive package, you fix it in this manifest's context (lockfile / overrides / constraint).
  - Two coupled findings (e.g. a package and its vulnerable transitive) get one consistent decision in the same PR — do not split them.
  - You may consult the org Knowledge notes attached to the session for install/build/test commands per ecosystem.
